title: Modern Geometric Headboard
summary: A plywood headboard panel overlaid with mitered 1x2 strips in a geometric pattern — a wood-grain design piece, distinct from this catalog's upholstered fabric headboards.
description:
This headboard is a design exercise rather than an upholstery project: a flat plywood panel gets skinned in strips of 1x2, each cut at 45° and laid down one at a time to build a geometric pattern across the surface — the opposite approach from this catalog's fabric-wrapped headboards, which hide their substrate entirely under padding. Lay the very first 1x2 flush and exactly parallel to one edge of the plywood before anything else — that first strip is the reference line every subsequent piece registers against, so getting it dead straight matters more than any single piece after it.

Work through the pattern strip by strip, mitering each 45° cut fresh against the piece before it rather than pre-cutting a batch, since the pattern's angles compound and a piece cut slightly off will show at the next joint. **Save the 1x4 vertical and top trim for last** — those pieces cap the geometric field on all sides and also catch any stray nail holes left in the plywood edge from the strips underneath, so they belong at the very end of the build, not partway through.

This headboard is sized to attach to a bed frame directly, but the plan includes a genuine wall-mount alternative: screw a 2x4 to the wall at 48" above the floor, sized to the headboard's width, then screw the headboard to that cleat along its top edge — a useful option if you don't want the headboard's weight riding entirely on the bed frame's own hardware.

tools.note: Cutting the plywood panel to size.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, full sheet, AC or hardwood recommended
Self-tapping wood screws, 3" and 2", for optional wall mount
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood, full sheet, AC or hardwood recommended
cutList.note: Sized for a queen; adjust width for other bed sizes.
Cut individually at 45° against the previous piece, not pre-cut as a batch.
Measure your assembled panel and cut to fit.

steps.title: Lay the reference strip
steps.body: Lay the plywood panel on a flat, level surface. Attach the first 1x2 strip flush and exactly parallel to the edge of the plywood, using 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue every 6-8".
materials: 3/4" plywood, full sheet, AC or hardwood recommended

steps.title: Build the geometric pattern
steps.body: Set the miter saw to 45°. Cut and place each subsequent strip against the piece before it, gluing and nailing down as you go.

steps.title: Continue the pattern
steps.body: Continue adding strips across the entire panel until the geometric field is complete.

steps.title: Add the vertical trim
steps.body: Cut the vertical side trim and attach to the sides of the panel with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, nailing into any strip ends you can reach.

steps.title: Add the top trim and finish
steps.body: Measure the top of the panel and cut the top trim to fit. Nail to the plywood, any strip ends, and the ends of the side trim. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mount to the wall (optional)
steps.body: If not attaching to a bed frame, screw the 2x4 cleat to the wall 48" above the floor, positioned where you want the headboard. Screw the headboard along its top edge into the cleat.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 3" and 2", for optional wall mount

unresolvedImages.alt: Plywood headboard with a mitered geometric wood pattern
